title: Delightful Polish Steak Stir-fry
description: A quick, herby Polish steak stir-fry brimming with fermented flavor and low-glycemic ingredients.
instructions: Heat sesame oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat until shimmering.
Add minced garlic and ginger to the skillet and sauté for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour in the lean minced steak, breaking it apart with a spatula, and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Stir in fermented soybean paste (ferment‑jang) and soy sauce, coating the meat evenly.
Add sliced bell pepper and zucchini, cooking until vegetables are tender‑crisp, about 4 minutes.
Season with chili flakes, black pepper, and a pinch of salt, tossing to combine flavors.
Slice the sautéed green onions and sprinkle over the stir‑fry just before serving.
Serve hot, optionally over cauliflower rice or a side of leafy greens for a low‑glycemic finish.
